name: arping
title: arping
summary: sends IP and/or ARP pings (to the MAC address)
description:
sends IP and/or ARP pings (to the MAC address)
The arping utility sends ARP and/or ICMP requests to the specified host
and displays the replies. The host may be specified by its hostname,
its IP address, or its MAC address.

commands.name: arping
commands.description: sends arp and/or ip pings to a given host

arguments.description: Only count addresses matching requested address (This *WILL* break most things you do.

arguments.description: Audible ping.

arguments.raw: -e  Like -a but beep when there is no reply.

arguments.description: Displays a help message and exits.
arguments.raw: -h  Displays a help message and exits.

arguments.description: Send ARP replies instead of requests. Useful with -U.
arguments.raw: -P  Send ARP replies instead of requests. Useful with -U.

arguments.description: Raw output: Like -r but shows "the other one", can be combined with -r.
arguments.raw: -R  Raw output: Like -r but shows "the other one", can be combined with -r.

arguments.description: Raw output: only the MAC/IP address is displayed for each reply.
arguments.raw: -r  Raw output: only the MAC/IP address is displayed for each reply.

arguments.description: Like -b and -0 but with set source address. Note that this may get the arping unan-
arguments.raw: -S  Like -b and -0 but with set source address. Note that this may get the arping unan-

arguments.description: Use -T as target address when pinging MACs that won't respond to a broadcast ping but
arguments.raw: -T  Use -T as target address when pinging MACs that won't respond to a broadcast ping but

arguments.description: Send unsolicited ARP. This sets the destination MAC address in
arguments.raw: -U  Send unsolicited ARP. This sets the destination MAC address in

arguments.description: Show index=received/sent instead of just index=received when
arguments.raw: -u  Show index=received/sent instead of just index=received when

arguments.description: Verbose output. Use twice for more messages.
arguments.raw: -v  Verbose output. Use twice for more messages.

command_examples: arping  [-0aAbBdDeFhpqrRuUv] [-S host/ip] [-T host/ip] [-s MAC]    [-t MAC] [-c count] [-i in-
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use arping because sends IP and/or ARP pings (to the MAC address).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use arping as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `arping  [-0aAbBdDeFhpqrRuUv] [-S host/ip] [-T host/ip] [-s MAC]    [-t MAC] [-c count] [-i in-` as documented upstream for arping.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `arping --help` as documented upstream for arping.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `ARPING $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST` as documented upstream for arping.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `ARPING 00:11:85:4c:01:01` as documented upstream for arping.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair arping with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `arping --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: arping - sends arp and/or ip pings to a given host
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install arping. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `arping  [-0aAbBdDeFhpqrRuUv] [-S host/ip] [-T host/ip] [-s MAC]    [-t MAC] [-c count] [-i in-`. Further invocations from upstream: `arping --help`; `ARPING $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`. sends IP and/or ARP pings (to the MAC address).
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: sends IP and/or ARP pings (to the MAC address).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: arping - sends arp and/or ip pings to a given host.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install arping
Install with: sudo apt install arping
